title: malus orientalis subsp. turkmenorum
intro: Malus orientalis subsp. turkmenorum, also known as the Turkmen apple, is a subspecies of the Malus orientalis species, which is a type of deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its various medicinal properties, including antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Malus orientalis subsp. turkmenorum include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and exhibit anti-cancer properties, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
